quiz_items.question: What accuracy is required for the radiation measurement system in Class IIIb/IV medical laser products?
quiz_items.explanation: Class IIIa, IIIb, and IV medical laser products must contain means for measuring delivered exposure accurate within ±20 percent, with recalibration procedures in the manual.

quiz_items.question: Demonstration laser products are permitted to exceed Class IIIa limits if they have proper warning labels.
quiz_items.explanation: False. Demonstration laser products are restricted to Class IIIa with Class I limits for short pulses and invisible wavelengths. Class IIIb/IV demonstration products require an approved variance.
